text:
CT CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 64-year-old female with known metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma, right kidney origin, status post right radical nephrectomy. Currently on cabozantinib 40 mg daily (reduced from 60 mg due to prior hand-foot syndrome). Study obtained for routine restaging; comparison made to prior CT dated approximately 3 months ago (the 6-month cabozantinib restaging study).

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis performed with intravenous contrast. 100 mL of Omnipaque 350 administered intravenously without complication. Portal venous phase imaging of the abdomen and pelvis and pulmonary vasculature phase of the chest were obtained. Multiplanar reformats reviewed. Lung windows, mediastinal windows, soft tissue windows, and bone windows reviewed.

COMPARISON: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast, prior restaging study approximately 3 months prior.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The lungs are well-aerated bilaterally. The airways are patent to the segmental level. No endobronchial lesion is identified.

The previously characterized right lower lobe pulmonary nodule has decreased in size, now measuring approximately 1.5 cm in its greatest axial dimension, compared with 1.8 cm on the prior examination. The nodule retains a rounded morphology with smooth margins and remains solid in attenuation without internal cavitation or calcification. Subtle peripheral enhancement is again noted, consistent with its known metastatic nature.

The right upper lobe pulmonary nodule has also decreased slightly, now measuring approximately 0.7 cm, compared with 0.9 cm previously. This lesion is subpleural in location and continues to demonstrate solid soft tissue attenuation.

In the left lower lobe, the previously described nodule is stable in size at approximately 1.1 cm, located in the posterior basal segment. It remains solid and round without interval change in character.

The scattered small bilateral pulmonary nodules previously identified - measuring up to 4 mm - appear stable to very slightly decreased in conspicuity when compared with the prior study. No new pulmonary nodules above the threshold of detection are identified on this examination.

No pleural effusions are present bilaterally. No pneumothorax. No evidence of pulmonary consolidation, ground-glass opacity, or airspace disease to suggest infection or inflammation. No atelectasis beyond trace dependent changes in the posterior lung bases bilaterally.

Mediastinum and Hila: The mediastinal and hilar contours are within normal limits. No mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy. The heart is not enlarged. No pericardial effusion. The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without dissection or aneurysmal change. Mild atherosclerotic calcification of the thoracic aorta, unchanged.

Chest Wall and Musculoskeletal Structures: No lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions are identified in the visualized thoracic spine, ribs, sternum, or shoulder girdles to suggest osseous metastatic disease. Mild degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with anterior osteophyte formation, unchanged.

---

ABDOMEN:

Nephrectomy Bed / Right Kidney: The right radical nephrectomy bed remains well-defined with expected postsurgical fibrotic change and fat stranding. There is no discrete soft tissue mass or abnormal enhancing tissue in the right retroperitoneum to suggest local recurrence at the resection site. The previously identified retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy has been resected and no residual nodal masses are identified in the right para-aortic or aortocaval regions above the aortic bifurcation. No new retroperitoneal adenopathy.

Left Kidney: The solitary left kidney is well-compensated and morphologically normal in contour. It measures approximately 12.1 cm in longitudinal dimension. No hydronephrosis. No perinephric fluid collection. No solid enhancing renal mass or cyst with concerning features (Bosniak I/II simple cysts noted, unchanged). The renal cortex enhances symmetrically without perfusion defect.

Liver: The liver is normal in size and homogeneous in attenuation. No focal hepatic lesion identified. The hepatic vasculature and bile ducts are normal. No biliary ductal dilation.

Gallbladder: Present, nondistended, without wall thickening, pericholecystic fluid, or cholelithiasis.

Pancreas: The pancreas is normal in size, contour, and attenuation throughout head, body, and tail. No pancreatic ductal dilation. No peripancreatic fluid.

Spleen: Normal in size. No focal splenic lesion.

Adrenal Glands: The left adrenal gland is normal in morphology without nodularity or mass. Given prior right nephrectomy, the right adrenal gland was not separately resected and is identified as a small triangular soft tissue structure in the right adrenal fossa; it appears stable and without significant enlargement or nodularity compared with prior imaging. No adrenal metastasis identified.

Aorta and Vasculature: The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber. No aneurysm. Mild infrarenal atherosclerotic calcification, stable from prior. The superior mesenteric artery, celiac axis, and inferior mesenteric artery origins appear patent.

Bowel: Small and large bowel are normal in caliber without obstruction, wall thickening, or mesenteric stranding. No free intraperitoneal air or fluid.

---

PELVIS:

Right Pelvic Sidewall Lymphadenopathy: The previously described right pelvic sidewall lymph node is again identified in the right obturator/internal iliac chain. It is stable in size at approximately 1.3 cm in short-axis dimension, unchanged from the prior examination. There is no interval increase in size, and the nodule does not demonstrate pathologically avid or necrotic change. This node has been persistently present since prior therapy and remains under surveillance.

Right External Iliac Lymphadenopathy: The right external iliac lymph node previously identified has decreased modestly in size, now measuring approximately 0.8 cm in short-axis dimension compared with 1.0 cm on the prior study. It remains soft tissue density without central necrosis.

No new pelvic sidewall, inguinal, or iliac lymphadenopathy is identified.

Bladder: The urinary bladder is partially distended and demonstrates a smooth, normal wall without intraluminal filling defect or mass. No perivesical fat stranding.

Gynecologic Structures: The uterus and bilateral adnexa are atrophic and unremarkable in appearance for the patient's age. No pelvic free fluid. No adnexal mass.

Muscular and Osseous Pelvis: No lytic or sclerotic bony lesion of the pelvic girdle, sacrum, or visualized proximal femora to suggest osseous metastatic disease. Mild bilateral hip osteoarthritis with joint space narrowing and marginal osteophyte formation, unchanged. Lumbar spine degenerative changes as described below.

Lumbar Spine: Multilevel spondylosis with mild-to-moderate degenerative disc disease at L3-L4 and L4-L5. No new compression fracture. No paraspinal soft tissue mass.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Right lower lobe pulmonary metastasis has modestly decreased in size from 1.8 cm to 1.5 cm since the prior examination, suggesting continued albeit gradual response to cabozantinib therapy. The right upper lobe pulmonary nodule similarly shows a modest interval reduction from 0.9 cm to 0.7 cm.

2. Left lower lobe pulmonary nodule is unchanged at 1.1 cm.

3. Scattered small bilateral pulmonary micronodules are stable and not convincingly changed. No new pulmonary lesions identified.

4. Right pelvic sidewall lymph node is stable at approximately 1.3 cm short axis, unchanged from the prior study.

5. Right external iliac lymph node has modestly decreased from 1.0 cm to 0.8 cm, consistent with continued treatment response at this site.

6. No new sites of metastatic disease are identified. Specifically, no hepatic, adrenal, osseous, or new nodal lesions are seen.

7. Right radical nephrectomy bed without evidence of locoregional recurrence. Solitary left kidney without concerning lesion.

8. Overall, the pattern is consistent with maintained disease control, with modest ongoing shrinkage in several lesions and stability in the remainder. The total measurable disease burden continues to be reduced compared with pre-cabozantinib baseline.

No acute intrathoracic, intraabdominal, or intrapelvic findings requiring emergent intervention.
